title: B002: Implement Kubernetes-interacting packages
summary: Review of changes addressing greptile-apps[bot] review #3 feedback. Three issues were addressed: (1) RunJob now detects stale completed Jobs by comparing pod template specs via apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual and performs delete-and-recreate when they differ, preventing skipped migrations on operator upgrades. (2) All 7 Ensure* functions (EnsureDatabase, ensureUser, ensureGrant, EnsureCertificate, EnsureDeployment, EnsurePushSecret, EnsureCronJob) now guard c.Update calls with an apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual check, eliminating unnecessary API churn. (3) Deployment readiness tests now explicitly set Generation/ObservedGeneration values and a new TestEnsureDeployment_notReadyWhenGenerationLags test exercises the safety guard directly.

tests_checklist.item: V1: All unit tests pass (go test ./database/... ./deployment/... ./job/... ./secrets/... ./tls/...)
TE1: Happy paths tested — TestRunJob_existingComplete_specUnchanged, TestEnsureDeployment_readyWhenAvailable with explicit Generation/ObservedGeneration
TE2: Edge cases tested — TestRunJob_existingComplete_specChanged (stale Job), TestEnsureDeployment_notReadyWhenGenerationLags (generation lag)
TE3: Error/failure paths tested — ErrJobFailed sentinel, spec drift detection
TE4: Tests independent — each test creates fresh fake client and objects
TE7: Assertions specific — verify exact image, owner ref count, readiness bool

code_quality_checklist.item: V2: go vet passes with 0 issues
Q1: Each function has single responsibility
Q3: apiequality guard pattern applied consistently via DRY principle
Q4: Clear naming — apiequality import alias consistent across all files
Q7: No dead code — no commented-out or unreachable code

security_checklist.item: S1: gosec scan: 0 issues across all changed files
S5: No sensitive data in error messages

architecture_checklist.item: C1: All three external review comments addressed correctly
C3: Edge cases handled (stale Jobs, generation lag, unchanged specs)
C5: No regression — existing tests still pass, build succeeds
PC3: apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual guard applied consistently across all 7 Ensure* update paths

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code — guard pattern is minimal and inline

fail_fast_checklist.item: IsJobFailed checked before IsJobComplete in RunJob
Stale Job spec drift detected before returning true

defensive_checklist.item: Re-fetch after update to avoid stale status evaluation
Owner reference set on replacement Job in RunJob stale path

suggested_improvements: D2 (minor): EnsureService (deployment/deployment.go:99) is the only Ensure* function without an apiequality guard. Its ClusterIP/NodePort preservation logic makes a pre-merge comparison less straightforward, but a post-merge comparison (comparing merged newSpec against existing.Spec before assignment) could skip the update when nothing changed. Not blocking since this function was not in scope of the external review feedback.

detected_patterns.name: Conditional spec update with apiequality guard
detected_patterns.applies_when: Writing an Ensure* function that creates-or-updates a Kubernetes resource by comparing desired spec against existing spec
detected_patterns.description: All Ensure* functions that perform create-or-update on Kubernetes resources guard the c.Update call with apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(existing.Spec, desired.Spec). When specs are equal, the update is skipped to avoid unnecessary API churn, spurious watch events, and 409 Conflict errors. When specs differ, the update is performed and the object is re-fetched to avoid evaluating stale status. The apiequality package is imported with the named alias 'apiequality'.

detected_patterns.name: Stale completed Job detection via pod template spec comparison
detected_patterns.applies_when: Implementing idempotent Job execution that must re-run when the Job spec changes (e.g., operator upgrade with new container image)
detected_patterns.description: RunJob guards against stale completed Jobs by comparing existing.Spec.Template.Spec against the desired job.Spec.Template.Spec using apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual after confirming IsJobComplete. When specs differ (e.g., new container image from an operator upgrade), the old Job is deleted and a new one is created with the updated spec, returning (false, nil) so the reconciler requeues. This prevents silently skipping migrations when the operator binary changes.

examples.code:
func TestRunJob_existingComplete_specChanged(t *testing.T) {
	g := NewGomegaWithT(t)
	s := newScheme()
	owner := testOwner()

	now := metav1.Now()
	oldJob := testJob()
	oldJob.Status.Succeeded = 1
	oldJob.Status.CompletionTime = &now
	oldJob.Status.Conditions = []batchv1.JobCondition{
		{Type: batchv1.JobComplete, Status: corev1.ConditionTrue},
	}

	c := fake.NewClientBuilder().
		WithScheme(s).
		WithObjects(owner, oldJob).
		WithStatusSubresource(oldJob).
		Build()

	newJob := testJob()
	newJob.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[0].Image = "busybox:v2"

	ready, err := RunJob(context.Background(), c, s, owner, newJob)
	g.Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
	g.Expect(ready).To(BeFalse(), "should recreate Job when spec changes after completion")
}
